content.problemStatement:
Archaeologist John and his team have unearthed the remains of an ancient civilisation. The partially destroyed walls of its houses contain strange pairs of numbers. John assumes that the first number in each pair always corresponds to a base (itself written in base 101010) and the second number in that pair is written in this base ― for example, `3 2110` denotes (2110)3=2⋅33+1⋅32+1⋅3+0=66(2110)_3 = 2 \cdot 3^3 + 1 \cdot 3^2 + 1 \cdot 3 + 0 = 66(2110)3​=2⋅33+1⋅32+1⋅3+0=66.

Unfortunately, the first number (the base) is often lost or unreadable. John made a list of pairs of numbers found on the walls, where he represents lost bases by −1-1−1. For example:

`-1 10001
3 2110
-1 18565
`John is wondering if all these pairs of numbers could represent a single common number ― that is, if it is possible to choose some number XXX in decimal representation (in base 101010) and fill in all missing bases in such a way that the second numbers in all pairs written on the walls, when converted from their bases to base 101010, are equal to XXX. For each pair with a missing base (−1,Y)(-1, Y)(−1,Y), we may choose any integer base BBB such that 2≤B≤362 \le B \le 362≤B≤36 and YYY is a valid number in base BBB.

Since John does not want to deal with huge numbers, he considers X>1012X \gt 10^{12}X>1012 to be invalid. Still, there are too many possibilities, so he needs your help. Your task is to find the smallest common number XXX that satisfies all conditions, or determine that no such common number exists.
constraints: YYY contains only digits '0' through '9' and letters 'A' through 'Z'
for each pair (B,Y)(B, Y)(B,Y) on the input, if B≠−1B \neq -1B=−1, YYY is a valid number in base BBB
the length of string YYY is at most 404040
Subtasks**Subtask #1 (30 points):** there is at least one pair (B,Y)(B, Y)(B,Y) such that B≠−1B \neq -1B=−1
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
Each of the following NNN lines contains an integer BBB followed by a space and a string YYY, describing a pair of numbers found on the walls; in the string YYY, digits 101010 through 353535 (in base 101010) are represented by letters 'A' through 'Z'.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the smallest possible common number XXX, or −1-1−1 if there is no valid common number.
